chunks.text: Hybrid Systems Laboratory This lab is under the direction of Professor Claire Tomlin in the Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS) Department at UC Berkeley. We study a range of problems in control theory and robotics that can be modeled

in control theory and robotics that can be modeled by hybrid systems -- a broad class of dynamical systems which exhibit both discrete event and continuous-time dynamics. We are particularly interested in designing and theoretically verifying control

d in designing and theoretically verifying control strategies for safety-critical autonomous systems. Though often theoretically-oriented, our research affects a variety of application areas, including air traffic control automation, algorithms for d

g air traffic control automation, algorithms for decentralized optimization, modeling and analysis of biological cell networks, and unmanned aerial vehicle design and control. Learn more about our projects and meet the people in the lab. Projects Peo

jects and meet the people in the lab. Projects People Safety Analysis Hamilton-Jacobi (HJ) reachability analysis, risk-sensitive safety analysis, safety analysis of robotic motion planners Control Safe controller synthesis, optimal control for nonlin

e controller synthesis, optimal control for nonlinear systems, feedback control for constrained systems Multi-Agent Systems Multi-unmanned aerial vehicle systems, human-robot interaction, motion planning for multi-agent robotic systems Machine Learni

ing for multi-agent robotic systems Machine Learning Reinforcement learning for contorl, verification of deep neural networks, learning dynamics, learning-based perception with model-based control Power Systems Renewable energy, electric distribution

er Systems Renewable energy, electric distribution networks Robotics Safe real-time motion planning, unmanned aerial vehicles, autonomous ground vehicles, gasping and manipulation, insect-scale robotics Biological Systems Breast cancer as a safety-cr
